topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on the screen, a stark intrusion into the quiet gray of my evening. A profile picture loaded: a grinning face I hadn't seen in fifteen years, the same cruel smirk I remembered from the relentless torment of middle school. My breath hitched, a shallow, painful catch in my chest. It was him. A friend request. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. Every instinct screamed at me to delete it, to bury the message under layers of forgotten junk, but a strange, heavy inertia kept my thumb frozen. I stared at the 'Accept' button as if it were a landmine, the silence in my small apartment amplifying the frantic thumping beneath my ribs. What did accepting this mean? Did it invite the old shadows back into the light? I finally closed the app, the resulting emptiness settling over me like a cold, suffocating blanket.

[story 2]

I didn't look up from the textbook, the words blurring into meaningless shapes on the page. The phone buzzed—a familiar, irritating sound that instantly tightened something painful in my stomach. It was a request from Mark. My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I squeezed my eyes shut, trying to will the sensation away, to make the buzzing stop. The thought of interacting with him felt like wading through thick mud; every step required an exhausting expenditure of energy I simply didn't possess right now. I pushed the phone face down onto the desk, the plastic cool against my palm. A wave of profound weariness washed over me, heavy and inescapable, making the simple act of breathing feel like a monumental effort. I just wanted the quiet to stay, undisturbed.

[story 3]

The sudden chime yanked me from a half-formed thought about the rain outside. It was an alert, a digital summons from a ghost from my past. Seeing his name pop up felt like a physical jolt, a sudden, unwelcome surge of anxiety that made my skin prickle. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric groaning under my weight, and let my gaze drift towards the window, watching the streetlights smear across the wet pavement. My hands began to shake, a subtle, involuntary tremor that betrayed the stillness of my insides. Why would he reach out now? The sheer audacity of the gesture made my throat constrict. I felt utterly hollowed out, as if some vital core had been scooped out and left behind. I didn't move to check the message; I just sat there, suspended in a state of heavy, unresponsive stillness.
